Shah Rukh Khan

Shah Rukh Khan, also known as SRK, is an Indian actor, film producer, and television personality. Referred to in the media as the "Baadshah of Bollywood" and "King Khan", he has appeared in more than 100 films, and earned numerous accolades, including 14 Filmfare Awards.

Early life and family

Khan was born on 2 November 1965 in New Delhi, India. His father, Meer Taj Mohammed Khan, was an Indian independence activist from Peshawar, who ran a small transport company. His mother, Lateef Fatima, was the adopted daughter of Major General Shah Nawaz Khan.

Acting career

Khan made his Bollywood debut in 1992 with Deewana, and was noted for his unconventional choice of roles. He played villainous characters in Darr (1993) and Baazigar (1993), which established his career.
